name: Fannin County Teachers
description_short: Fannin County Teachers Federal Credit Union is a well-capitalized community credit union serving educators in Bonham, Texas with $10.4M in assets and 1,132 members.
description_long:
Fannin County Teachers Federal Credit Union was chartered in 1969 and operates as a multiple common bond credit union with a primary focus on serving educators in the Fannin County area. Based in Bonham, Texas, the institution has grown to serve 1,132 members with approximately $10.4 million in total assets as of Q1 2024. The credit union maintains a single branch location and is led by CEO Pamela D. Compton, operating with a lean team of 2 full-time employees. As a federally chartered credit union, it is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and offers member deposits protection through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF).

Fannin County Teachers FCU provides core banking and lending services to its membership, including deposit accounts, auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans. Members can access accounts through the single branch location at 621 N Center Street in Bonham. The institution reported Q1 2024 net interest income of $115,926 and maintained $7.7 million in total shares and deposits. The credit union offers competitive member services typical of small community financial institutions, with interest-bearing share accounts and loan products designed for the educator community it serves.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its exceptionally strong capitalization position, with a net worth ratio of 25.41—significantly above the 7.0 threshold required for "well-capitalized" status. This conservative capital management reflects prudent financial stewardship. The institution's focus on a defined field of membership (educators) creates a tight-knit community where underwriting decisions may benefit from understanding members' employment stability and financial profiles. With zero ATMs and minimal staffing, this is intentionally an intimate, relationship-based institution rather than a high-technology operation.

However, this credit union is best suited for a very specific demographic. With no ATM network and only one branch location, members in other areas will face significant access limitations. The small asset size and single-branch model mean limited product sophistication compared to larger financial institutions. This is a small, traditional credit union ideal for local educators seeking personalized service, but impractical for those requiring modern digital banking infrastructure or nationwide access. Fee income was minimal at $695 for Q1 2024, suggesting limited fee-based service offerings. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking and savings accounts with interest-bearing share accounts
Auto loans for new and used vehicle financing
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinancing
NCUA share insurance protection up to $250,000 per member account
Loan services with underwriting based on member employment stability
Member financial counseling through credit union relationship managers
Deposit products including shares and deposits ($7.67M outstanding)
Credit union membership for educators and educational professionals
Account access through single branch location at 621 N Center Street
pros: Exceptionally well-capitalized with net worth ratio of 25.41, far exceeding the 7.0 threshold for well-capitalized status
Low delinquency risk suggested by allowance for loan losses of only $42,723 on $7.79M in total loans
Specialized field of membership (educators) provides community focus and relationship-based banking
Profitable operation with $40,265 net income in Q1 2024 and positive net interest margin of $115,926
Strong member base retention with 1,132 active members despite minimal marketing footprint
Federal charter provides NCUA insurance protection for member deposits
Conservative management demonstrated by $2.3M in cash and deposits relative to asset base
cons: Zero ATM locations and single branch operation severely limits member access and convenience
Very small institution ($10.4M assets) offers limited product complexity and loan approval flexibility compared to larger credit unions
Minimal staffing (2 full-time employees) may create service bottlenecks and limited hours of operation
Restricted field of membership (educators primarily) excludes most of the general population from membership eligibility
No digital banking infrastructure apparent—website is financial reporting only with no account management features mentioned
best_for: Educators employed in Fannin County, Texas seeking personalized relationship banking
Small business owners in education sector wanting to bank with mission-aligned institution
Members within close proximity to Bonham, TX branch who prioritize local community connection over convenience
Conservative savers seeking FDIC/NCUA-insured deposit accounts at a financially stable institution
diagnosis: Fannin County Teachers Federal Credit Union is best for educators employed in the Fannin County area who value personalized, relationship-based banking and community connection over technological convenience. The primary caveat is that this small, single-branch institution offers no ATM network and minimal digital infrastructure, making it impractical for members requiring modern banking access, geographic flexibility, or sophisticated financial products—it is fundamentally a local institution for a defined local membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
